creator: lsim4242@gmail.com
text:
Hi there, I'm currently running:

FreeBSD <Hostname> 10.0-RELEASE-p4 FreeBSD 10.0-RELEASE-p4 #0: Tue Jun  3 13:14:57 UTC 2014     root@amd64-builder.daemonology.net:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  amd64

and I think the variable 'vfs.unmapped_buf_allowed' is also responsible for a perl core dump which I am facing at the moment.

I'm not running a virtualised OS - just a straight forward OS on a Zeon machine.

Now the nitty gritties - I was running a big job (mixture of perl, c++, sh codes)
that took about 5 hours to run. Normally on previous OS versions, the job will run fine. However, on Freebsd 10.0 one of the perl jobs will core-dump near the
end of its run. 

I tried, narrowing down on the problem by (a) reducing the data size for the job, (b) skipping the many stages of the job but when I did any of these, the core dump disappears!

When I did added more print statements into the Perl code (that was affected),
the core dump disappears!

I finally managed to replicate the problem but had to live with a 3hr turn around time to see of the core dump will happen.

After many days of testing, I found that it was not related to hardware, input data or the code itself (as the core dump position will move about in the perl code). I even thought it was the 'soft update' for the FS - nope.

Eventually, on desperation, I turned the variable 'vfs.unmapped_buf_allowed' to 0, and presto, the core dump disappears and all is well again!

This is just a cautionary note for anyone experiencing very rare random core dumps on Freebsd 10.0.

Oh Yes - I ran the script below and could not duplicate the that corruption
even with 'vfs.unmapped_buf_allowed' set to 1

Maybe mine is a new bug :-( hopefully not! But hope this helps the developer.

sim
